name: Yael to Daxis
description: The Crystal Spire is a Grid node that never shut down. Scholar Daxis at the Resonance Core has three years of emission data from the primary scar. The Spire's continuous-output data would complete her propagation model.
accept_text: She has been preparing the data package since before you asked — a researcher who has been maintaining records without a recipient for three months moves very quickly when a recipient appears. 'Three months of continuous emission data from the Spire,' she says. 'The raw readings, the correlation with Karee's timing intervals, and my analysis of the pulse acceleration over the eight-month network healing period.' She closes the case. 'Daxis is at the Resonance Core. I know her work — I've read her pre-Cascade emission analysis publications. If she has the scar data and I have the Spire data, the combination would give us both ends of the original propagation sequence.' She pauses. 'The Cascade propagated from the Core outward. The Spire was beyond the propagation path — it never received the inversion wave. So we'd have the source of the Cascade and the unaffected node that the Cascade didn't reach, with three hundred years of data between them.' She looks at the instruments. 'Her reconstruction of the inversion wave and my continuous output record would let us calculate the full original propagation model — what the Grid looked like before the Cascade, and what it will look like when it finishes healing.' She hands you the case. 'Tell her: the Spire has been broadcasting for three hundred years. Tell her the Cascade missed it. Tell her I've been on the receiving end of the recovery signal for eight months without knowing what I was receiving it from.' She pauses. 'And ask her if she wants a collaborator. I think we're working on the same problem from different positions.'
complete_text: Daxis takes the case and opens it standing up, which is how she processes things that require her full attention. She reads the front page of Yael's analysis with the expression of someone whose model has just gained a crucial data point that resolves several outstanding problems simultaneously. 'The Spire.' She says it slowly. 'Three hundred years of uninterrupted construction wave output from a node the Cascade missed.' She looks at the numbers. 'This is why the scar's echo was accelerating. The Spire has been broadcasting continuously into the network, and the network has been building toward coherence not just from the reactivation event eight months ago — it's been building from the Spire's continuous output for three hundred years.' She sets the page down. 'The healing isn't recent. It's been happening since the Cascade. The Spire has been doing it slowly, in the background, for three centuries. Eight months ago the primary node reactivated and the healing accelerated.' She is very quiet. 'The recovery wasn't waiting for someone to trigger it. It was already in progress.' She looks at the scar. 'The Cascade happened and the Grid started healing immediately from the one node that survived. And three hundred years later we're here watching the last phase of something that began the day after the worst thing that happened in our history.' She pauses. 'Tell Yael: yes. Tell her I want a collaborator and I think we've been working on the same problem for three months from four kilometers apart.' She picks up a notebook. 'Tell her to come here. I want to show her what the scar looks like in person.'
